sections.paragraph: m. adj. salted. Isa 30:24, בְּלִיל חָמִיץ “salted provender,” i.e. sprinkled with salt; of which flocks and herds are so fond, that the Arabs say proverbially, sweet fodder (حلة) is the camels’ bread, when salted it is their sweetmeats. See Bochart, Hieroz. t. i. p. 113. Faber in Harmer’s Observations, vol. i. p. 409.
